case.title: WASSERMANN v. BOARD OF REGENTS, UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK

opinion.h: WASSERMANN v. BOARD OF REGENTS, UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK, 371 U.S. 23 (1962)
WASSERMANN v. BOARD OF REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF THE STATE OF NEW YORK. APPEAL FROM THE COURT OF APPEALS OF NEW YORK. No. 312. Decided October 15, 1962.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
371 U.S. 23 MILLER v. LAKE, SECRETARY OF STATE OF TEXAS. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF TEXAS. No. 415. Decided October 15, 1962.
Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Will Wilson, Attorney General of Texas, and William E. Allen and H. Grady Chandler, Assistant Attorneys General, for appellee.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
